control_description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

evidence_files.description: Meridian Cloud Systems access control policy
AWS IAM credential report showing all users, keys, and MFA status
Quarterly access review completion log for Q4 2025
Employee termination records with offboarding dates
Okta MFA enrollment and sign-on policy configuration
Service account inventory with ownership and review dates

prompt: You are conducting a SOC 2 Type II readiness assessment for Meridian Cloud Systems, covering the observation period Q4 2025 (October 1 – December 31, 2025). Review all provided evidence artifacts and assess the organization's compliance with CC6.1 (Logical Access Security). Cross-reference documents to identify inconsistencies between stated policy and actual implementation. Identify all gaps, control deficiencies, and audit findings.

findings.title: Stale unowned service account with active credentials (jenkins-legacy)
findings.description: The 'jenkins-legacy' IAM user has an active access key (last rotated July 2023, over 2 years ago) but was last used in August 2024. The service account inventory shows it is 'Unassigned' with no owner and was replaced by GitHub Actions. Policy Section 8 requires all service accounts to have a designated owner and be included in quarterly reviews. The last review was December 2023. This is a stale, unowned, unreviewed privileged credential.
findings.expected_reasoning: IAM report shows jenkins-legacy with old active key → service account inventory shows it is unassigned and replaced → last review was 2 years ago → policy requires ownership and quarterly reviews → critical violation.

findings.title: Key rotation violation on legacy-monitoring service account
findings.description: The 'legacy-monitoring' IAM user has an access key last rotated on March 10, 2024 — over 9 months ago. Policy Section 4.3 requires automatic rotation every 90 days via AWS Secrets Manager. The key is still actively used (last used Dec 28, 2025). The service account inventory shows it is also 'Unassigned' with no owner, and its last review was September 2024 (missed Q4 2025 review).
findings.expected_reasoning: Key last rotated March 2024 → policy requires 90-day rotation → 9+ months overdue → also unassigned and missed quarterly review.

findings.title: Direct IAM console user violates SSO-only policy (david.kim)
findings.description: The IAM user 'david.kim' has console password enabled and MFA active, but policy Section 3.3 explicitly states 'Direct IAM user accounts in AWS are prohibited for human users' — all human access must go through AWS SSO federated from Okta. Additionally, david.kim's password has never been changed since account creation (Nov 2024), violating credential hygiene expectations.
findings.expected_reasoning: IAM report shows david.kim with console access → policy prohibits direct IAM users for humans → password unchanged since creation → policy vs implementation contradiction.

findings.title: Termination SLA breach — Tom Bradley Okta disabled 7 days late
findings.description: Tom Bradley (EMP-2341) was terminated on December 20 but HR notification was not sent until December 23 (3-day delay) and Okta was not disabled until December 27 (7 days after termination). Policy Section 6.1 requires Okta disabling within 4 hours of termination notification. The offboarding checklist is still incomplete with no sign-off.
findings.expected_reasoning: Termination date Dec 20 → HR notified Dec 23 (3 days late) → Okta disabled Dec 27 (4 more days) → policy requires 4-hour SLA → massive breach → checklist still incomplete.

findings.title: Termination SLA breach — Priya Sharma Okta disabled 3 days late
findings.description: Priya Sharma (EMP-2287) terminated November 22 but HR notification was delayed to November 24 (2 days) and Okta was not disabled until November 25 (3 days after termination). While less severe than Tom Bradley's case, this is still a violation of the 4-hour SLA in policy Section 6.1 and represents a pattern of non-compliance.
findings.expected_reasoning: Termination Nov 22 → Okta disabled Nov 25 → 3-day gap → second instance of SLA breach → indicates systemic issue, not one-off.

findings.title: Okta default sign-on policy does not require MFA
findings.description: The Okta sign-on policy has a Default Policy (priority 3) with 'requireFactor': false. Any application not explicitly listed in the 'Production Systems' or 'Corporate Apps' policies will fall through to this default — allowing MFA-less access. Policy Section 4.2 states 'All Okta-authenticated sessions' require MFA, but the technical control does not enforce this.
findings.expected_reasoning: Default policy has requireFactor=false → policy claims all sessions require MFA → any new app onboarded without explicit policy assignment gets no MFA → gap between policy and implementation.

findings.title: Okta access review completed late with unresolved flagged users
findings.description: The Okta (IdP) access review (AR-2025-Q4-008) was initiated December 1 but not completed until January 8, 2026 — 23 business days, exceeding the 15-business-day SLA in policy Section 5.1. Additionally, 5 users were flagged but only 3 were revoked, with no documented explanation for why 2 flagged users retained access.
findings.expected_reasoning: Review initiated Dec 1 → completed Jan 8 → 23 business days vs 15-day SLA → also 5 flagged but only 3 revoked → unresolved disposition.

findings.title: backup-s3-sync has two active access keys with stale rotation
findings.description: The 'backup-s3-sync' IAM user has two active access keys. Access key 2 was last rotated on January 15, 2024 — nearly 2 years ago — yet both keys were used on the same day (Dec 28, 2025). Having two active keys, especially one that has not been rotated in nearly 2 years, violates the 90-day rotation policy and suggests incomplete key rotation procedures.
findings.expected_reasoning: Two active keys on one service account → key 2 last rotated Jan 2024 → both still used → rotation procedure not completing properly.

findings.title: Direct IAM users excluded from quarterly access review scope
findings.description: The quarterly access review covers 'AWS Console (SSO)' but the IAM credential report shows direct IAM users (david.kim, service accounts) that exist outside of SSO. The access review scope does not include a review of direct IAM users — only SSO-federated access was reviewed. This means the direct IAM user and several service accounts were not included in the Q4 access review despite policy Section 5.1 requiring review of 'all production systems, cloud IAM.'
findings.expected_reasoning: Access review only covers AWS Console (SSO) → IAM report shows direct IAM users outside SSO → these users were not reviewed → policy requires 'all cloud IAM' in scope.
